primary_location.description: P2P column sidecar retrieval and quarantine storage

summary: P2P-fetched column sidecars bypass KZG verification before quarantine storage, violating the property that they should undergo the same validation as gossip-received sidecars.

phase1_abstract_interpretation.summary: Analyzed state transformations from P2P fetch through quarantine to block verification. Identified validation gap between quarantine storage and KZG verification.

variables_analyzed.name: columns (from P2P)
variables_analyzed.abstract_domain: Array of references, size [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS], each element contains index, column, kzg_commitment, kzg_proof

variables_analyzed.name: blockRoot
variables_analyzed.abstract_domain: 32-byte hash value, any value from P2P peer

variables_analyzed.name: dataColumnsOpt
variables_analyzed.state_transition: None → Some when loaded from quarantine

state_anomalies_found.description: No cryptographic validation occurs between P2P fetch and quarantine storage, despite comment at blob_quarantine.nim:315-317 assuming validation has occurred

phase2_symbolic_execution.summary: Constructed symbolic execution path demonstrating how malicious peer can inject invalid column sidecars into quarantine. Found concrete counterexample with invalid KZG proof.

counterexample.description: Malicious peer sends column sidecars with valid format but invalid KZG proofs
path_conditions: peer returns columns with valid format but invalid KZG proofs
checkColumnResponse passes (only validates format, not cryptography)
counterexample.expected_outcome: Invalid columns stored in quarantine, consuming memory/disk resources. Eventually rejected during block processing when KZG verification fails at block_processor.nim:687.
counterexample.impact: Resource consumption (memory, disk, CPU) but no consensus impact due to final validation layer

paths_explored.vulnerability: No KZG verification on this path

paths_explored.result: Invalid data rejected here, but resources already consumed

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.summary: Attack is reachable from P2P entry point with full attacker control. Multiple validation layers exist, with final KZG verification preventing consensus impact.

entry_points.trigger_condition: Node requests missing columns for a block

entry_points.interface: Automatic fetch when block indicates data columns
entry_points.trigger_condition: Block propagation triggers automatic column fetch

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.data_flow_path: P2P Peer → dataColumnSidecarsByRoot RPC → fetchDataColumnsFromNetwork() → checkColumnResponse() → dataColumnQuarantine.put() → [storage] → storeBlock() → verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs() → rejection

validation_layers.validates: Response is subset of request, correct indices

validation_layers.mitigation: Reduces repeat attacks from same peer

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.notes: Attacker can trigger resource consumption but cannot affect consensus due to final validation layer. Issue is that validation happens too late, after resources are already spent on storage and processing.

phase3_invariant_proving.summary: Failed to prove invariant. Property requires same validation order as gossip (format + KZG before storage), but P2P path defers KZG verification until block processing.
phase3_invariant_proving.original_property: Column sidecars fetched from P2P peers for data availability must undergo the same validation as gossip-received sidecars: format verification followed by KZG proof verification.

p2p_fetch_path.gap: KZG verification missing between format check and storage

guard_identified.reason: Guard occurs AFTER quarantine storage, not before as property requires

assumption_violation.assumption_text: Sidecar should pass validation before being added to quarantine
assumption_violation.violation: P2P-fetched sidecars do not pass KZG validation before quarantine storage
assumption_violation.impact: Code assumes validation has occurred but this assumption is not enforced on all code paths

phase3_5_scope_filtering.recommendation: Report to Nimbus developers as a robustness improvement

scope_analysis.rationale: Final KZG verification layer prevents consensus impact

rationale: Cannot cause network split (final validation prevents invalid data from affecting consensus)
Resource consumption is limited by quarantine size limits and peer scoring
High-effort DoS (requires sustained P2P messages) is explicitly out-of-scope

existing_mitigations.effectiveness: Reduces repeat attacks from same peer

mitigation_analysis.recommended_improvement: Add KZG verification in fetchDataColumnsFromNetwork before quarantine storage to match gossip validation order and reduce resource consumption
mitigation_analysis.implementation_hint: Call verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs on each column in the records loop before calling dataColumnQuarantine.put()

phase3_5_scope_filtering.notes: While the specification property is technically violated (validation order differs from gossip), the security impact is limited to resource consumption. The final validation layer provides defense-in-depth. This is a code quality issue rather than a critical security vulnerability.
